circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit is a 5. 281-kilometre track on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi, designed by Hermann Tilke, and has served as the venue for Formula One's season finale since 2009. The circuit represents the second of four Middle Eastern Formula One venues, following Bahrain and preceding those in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: A street circuit encircling Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, this venue hosts the Formula One Australian Grand Prix each year alongside various supporting motorsport events. The track holds an FIA Grade 1 classification.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: The 4. 326 km circuit at Spielberg in Austria's Styria region has hosted the Austrian Grand Prix across two distinct eras, first under its original Österreichring name from 1970 to 1987, then following a redesign and renaming as the A1-Ring from 1997 to 2003. The track underwent significant modifications between these periods, including a shortening and reconstruction of the layout.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan spanning 6. 003 km, ranking as the fourth-longest venue in Formula One. The circuit hosted its first F1 race as the 2016 European Grand Prix, before being renamed the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit spans 5. 412 kilometers and held the first Formula One Grand Prix ever staged in the Middle East that same year. The FIA Grade 1 venue accommodates various motorsport categories including GP2 racing and endurance competitions, featuring multiple circuit configurations for different events.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometres and features a versatile layout combining extended straights with diverse corner configurations, making it a comprehensive test of driver and car capability. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates up to 140,700 spectators across its grandstand facilities.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: The Belgian Grand Prix takes place at this 7. 004 km circuit nestled in the Walloon region near Spa. One of Formula One's most historic venues, it first hosted a Grand Prix in 1925 and has been a regular fixture on the championship calendar since 1985, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, Quebec, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361 km temporary street circuit that serves as the home of the Formula One Canadian Grand Prix. Beyond hosting Formula One, the venue has accommodated various other racing series including international sportscar championships, North American open-wheel and stock car competitions.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit in China's Jiading District has been the primary home of the Chinese Grand Prix, hosting the event from 2004 until 2019 before resuming from 2024. The venue has expanded its motorsport calendar to include Formula E racing from 2024 onwards.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located near Silverstone in Northamptonshire, this English circuit inaugurated the British Grand Prix in 1948 and hosted the inaugural World Championship race two years later. After rotating among three venues during the 1955-1986 period, the grand prix became a permanent fixture here from 1987 onwards.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, a 4. 381 km circuit located near Budapest in Hungary, hosted the inaugural Formula One Grand Prix held within the Eastern Bloc in 1986, making it a historic venue for the sport. Built in just eight months starting from October 1985, faster than any other Formula One track at the time, the purpose-built facility was constructed near a major highway after the government rejected plans for a street circuit in Budapest's central park.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza, located near Milan in northern Italy, is a 5. 793 km circuit constructed in 1922 as Europe's oldest purpose-built racing venue. Home to the Italian Grand Prix continuously since 1949, the track earns its nickname "Temple of Speed" from its characteristic long straights and fast corners that encourage high-velocity racing.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, operated by Honda Mobilityland in Mie Prefecture, Japan, spans 5. 807 kilometers and hosts major motorsport events with a capacity of 155,000 spectators. The venue is primarily renowned for staging Formula One races and Japanese Super Formula competitions.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, following the famous Las Vegas Boulevard and its iconic resort corridor. The layout winds past landmark casinos and hotels such as Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, as well as the distinctive MSG Sphere venue, creating a uniquely urban racing environment.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Mexico City's Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit named in tribute to brothers Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both fatal racing casualties in the 1960s and 1970s. The venue has hosted the Formula One Mexican Grand Prix across three separate eras, with its current hosting period beginning in 2015 after a 23-year absence, now officially branded as the Mexico City Grand Prix since 2021.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary road course constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens that measures 3. 363 miles with 19 corners, allowing qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph. Designed specifically for Formula One racing by Apex Circuit Design, the venue hosted the Miami Grand Prix beginning in the 2022 season.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco stretches 3. 337 kilometers through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, following a route around Monaco's harbor. Though officially named after the circuit itself, the track is widely known as "Monte Carlo" since the majority of its layout occupies that particular district of the principality.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: This 4. 259 km Dutch racetrack situated in coastal dunes near Amsterdam has hosted Formula One's Dutch Grand Prix since its 2021 return to the championship calendar, though the partnership concludes following the 2026 season. The venue, located in the North Sea region of the Netherlands, underwent a naming agreement beginning in mid-2025.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: Located north of Doha in Qatar, Lusail International Circuit spans 5. 419 kilometres with a prominent 1. 068-kilometre main straight and holds FIA Grade 1 certification.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Located in São Paulo, this 4. 309 km Brazilian motorsport venue opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace, who tragically died in an aircraft accident eight years earlier. The anticlockwise track hosts major racing events and features an adjacent kart facility dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street racing venue positioned along Saudi Arabia's Red Sea waterfront that has hosted the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since 2021, while also beginning to accommodate Formula E competitions from 2025 onwards.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit is a temporary racing venue laid out through Singapore's Marina Bay district, incorporating sections of the city's Downtown Core and Kallang neighborhoods.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: The Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya, located in Montmeló near Barcelona, stretches 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced mix of extended straights and diverse corner configurations that test multiple racing skills. The track holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that hosts the Formula One United States Grand Prix alongside major motorcycle and endurance racing events. The venue has served as home to numerous racing series including NASCAR, MotoGP, and various sports car championships throughout its history.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai and British motorsport competitor who drives for the Williams team in Formula One, racing under Thailand's national flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso competes for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has claimed two world championships with Renault in 2005 and 2006 alongside 32 Grand Prix victories over 23 seasons. Beyond single-seaters, he has demonstrated exceptional versatility in endurance racing, winning the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, two Le Mans 24 Hours titles with Toyota, and the 2019 Daytona 24 Hours, making him the sole driver to capture both an F1 championship and a world sportscar/endurance title.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Andrea Kimi Antonelli represents Italy in Formula One racing with Mercedes, having claimed five Grand Prix victories since entering the sport in 2025.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Haas, where he represents the team in the championship.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Gabi Bortoleto, a Brazilian racing driver, currently competes in Formula One as part of the Audi team.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Valtteri Bottas is a Finnish driver competing in Formula One for Cadillac. Over his 13-season career, he has secured two runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship while with Mercedes and has claimed 10 race victories.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Argentine racing driver Franco Alejandro Colapinto currently races in Formula One with the Alpine team, bringing his skills to one of the sport's established organizations.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan has taken part in seven Formula One races since 2024 while holding a reserve role with Haas. Beyond his F1 commitments, he actively competes in European endurance racing with Nielsen Racing in the LMP2 category.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly, a French racing driver, currently competes in Formula One for Alpine. The driver achieved a notable victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while racing for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar represents France in Formula One, competing for Red Bull Racing. The driver holds both French and Algerian nationality.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari who shares the record for most championship titles with seven wins, matching Michael Schumacher's achievement. He holds multiple all-time records in the sport, including the greatest number of race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: Nicolas Hülkenberg is a German Formula One driver currently racing for Audi. Beyond his single-seater commitments, he achieved victory in the 24 Hours of Le Mans during 2015 while driving for Porsche in endurance competition.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand motorsport competitor currently racing in Formula One for the Racing Bulls team.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc represents Monaco in Formula One, driving for Ferrari where he has established himself as a competitive force. The 2022 season saw him finish as championship runner-up, and he has accumulated eight race victories during his nine-year career in the sport.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen, a Danish driver known as K-Mag, has stepped away from full-time Formula One racing following stints between 2014-2020 and 2022-2024, now focusing on endurance competition where he represents BMW with WRT in the FIA World Endurance Championship. He also pursues part-time involvement in American motorsport, piloting the No. 91 Chevrolet for Trackhouse Racing in the NASCAR Cup Series.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver competing for McLaren, claimed the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship in his eighth season with the team. The multiple race winner has secured 11 Grand Prix victories throughout his career in the sport.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The Monaco-born competitor previously secured a Grand Prix victory at the 2021 Hungarian event while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Checo Pérez, a Mexican driver, currently races for Cadillac in Formula One after spending 15 seasons in the sport. He finished as championship runner-up in 2023 while competing for Red Bull and has secured six Grand Prix victories throughout his career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Australian driver Oscar Piastri currently races for McLaren in Formula One, having secured nine Grand Prix victories since joining the series four seasons ago.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo is an Australian driver who raced in Formula One between 2011 and 2024, earning the nickname "the Honey Badger" during his 14-season career. He secured eight Grand Prix victories throughout his time in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, having secured seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-year career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: The Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes in Formula One for Williams and has secured four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver who raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to sports car racing. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition, following prior experience in IMSA's LMP2 prototype class.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian driver currently competing in Formula One for the Aston Martin team.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver who held Formula One positions spanning 2021 through 2025, currently functioning as a reserve driver for Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: The Dutch-Belgian driver competes for Red Bull Racing and has secured four consecutive world championship titles between 2021 and 2024. Across his 12 seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories, establishing himself as one of the most successful drivers in modern Formula One history.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu, a Chinese racing driver, holds the distinction of being the sole driver from China to participate in Formula One competition. After racing in the sport from 2022 through 2024, he has transitioned to a reserve role with Cadillac.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based Alpine team has competed in Formula One since 2021 as the rebranded works squad of the French automotive group Renault, following the company's decision to promote its Alpine sports car division. Operating with a French racing licence and powered by Mercedes engines, the team achieved its only victory in the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix through Esteban Ocon before Renault's departure from the sport at the end of 2025.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has competed in Formula One through different organizational structures, with its current iteration operating as AMR GP Limited under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Ferrari's racing division, nicknamed "the Prancing Horse," stands as Formula One's longest-running and most triumphant team, maintaining continuous participation in the World Championship since its inaugural 1950 season. As the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, Scuderia Ferrari has established itself as the sport's most enduring competitor and successful organization.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: An American-licensed Formula One team founded by NASCAR team owner Gene Haas and launched in 2016, the outfit has been led by team principal Ayao Komatsu since early 2024, following the departure of Guenther Steiner who held the position from its establishment.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Sauber Motorsport AG was a Swiss racing operation founded in 1970 that competed in Formula One from 1993 onwards, becoming one of the sport's longest-serving constructors before ceasing competition in 2025. The team achieved significant success in endurance racing, including victory at Le Mans in 1989, and underwent various transformations throughout its Grand Prix career. Audi acquired the operation and its infrastructure in 2024 to establish the foundation for its own Formula One programme.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren is a British Formula One outfit headquartered at its purpose-built facility in Woking that competes under corporate sponsorship. The team operates as part of the wider McLaren Group, which is controlled by Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: The Mercedes-AMG Petronas team, based in Brackley and holding a German racing licence, represents Mercedes-Benz's ongoing involvement in Formula One since 1954 as both constructor and engine supplier. From January 2022, the squad operates under a tripartite ownership structure shared equally between Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and team principal Toto Wolff. The outfit carries the historical "Silver Arrows" designation, a name synonymous with Mercedes' racing heritage.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor that entered the sport in 2024 as part of Red Bull's two-team structure. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility near Milton Keynes, the squad operates as Red Bull's sister team alongside the primary Red Bull Racing outfit, both controlled by the Austrian Red Bull conglomerate.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that commenced competition in 2024, operating as part of Red Bull GmbH's two-team setup alongside Red Bull Racing. Based in Faenza with additional facilities in Milton Keynes, the squad represents the Austrian conglomerate's second constructor entry in the championship.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, operating under an Austrian licence from its English base, emerged as a Formula One competitor in 2005 and has become one of two racing operations owned by Red Bull GmbH, the other being Racing Bulls. The team was led by Christian Horner throughout its two-decade existence until his 2025 departure, when Laurent Mekies assumed the leadership role.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, succeeding Williams's prior failed racing venture. Based in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team competes in 2026 under the Atlassian Williams F1 Team name.
